data.title: $100M hedge fund by Galaxy to invest in rising/falling crypto prices and traditional stocks, moving away from "up-only" approach.
data.description: Galaxy is preparing to implement a novel hedge fund approach that focuses on crypto tokens and conventional financial stocks as the up-only trend wanes. This strategic shift comes at a time when investors are diversifying their portfolios and looking for new opportunities. By combining these two asset classes, Galaxy aims to capitalize on the market trends and maximize returns. This move is significant as it marks a departure from the previous strategy, signaling a bold move towards a more balanced and dynamic investment approach. Investors are closely watching this development as it could potentially reshape the investment landscape.

data.title: Galaxy preps $100M hedge fund for crypto and traditional assets, shifting strategy amid market changes.
data.description: Galaxy is set to implement a novel hedge fund approach focusing on a combination of crypto tokens and traditional financial stocks as the enthusiasm for an "only-up" market stage diminishes.

data.title: Samsung Biologics had a successful Q4 in 2025, with revenue hitting KRW 1,286 billion. FY'25 revenue stood at KRW 4,557 billion, showing strong growth potential.
data.description: The revenue for the fourth quarter of 2025 was reported at KRW 1,286 billion, with the total revenue for the fiscal year 2025 reaching KRW 4,557 billion. The company sustained its performance momentum by meeting operational targets and meeting high client demand. It further enhanced its prospects for long-term growth by increasing manufacturing preparedness and operational capacity.
